verdict_reasoning: HSPA1A (heat shock protein A1A / HSP70) is a canonical cytoplasmic chaperone with dominant intracellular localization. However, it has documented surface display under stress and cancer contexts: (1) cell-state induction — heat shock, ER stress, and oxidative stress drive HSP70 translocation to the plasma membrane outer leaflet where it functions in danger signaling and immune recognition; (2) cancer ecto-display — tumor cells constitutively expose HSP70 on the outer surface via both canonical stress-response pathways and oncogene-driven ecto-translocation, where it acts as a tumor-associated antigen and ligand for pattern-recognition receptors (TLR2, TLR4, LOX-1, CD40). (3) Cell-death induction — apoptotic and necroptotic cells externalize HSP70 to the outer leaflet as a damage-associated molecular pattern. Surface HSP70 is the target of therapeutic antibodies, CAR-T programs, and checkpoint modulators in oncology. The baseline compartment is cytoplasmic/ER-associated, but non-baseline stress-state and cancer-cell ecto-pools are well-documented and clinically relevant.
